text: An eye for an eye only ends up making the whole world blind.
context: Core statement of the philosophy of non-violence (ahimsa).

text: There is no path to peace. Peace is the path.
context: Emphasizing that non-violence is both means and end.

text: I will not let anyone walk through my mind with their dirty feet.
context: Refusing to internalize the contempt of the oppressor.

text: The weak can never forgive. Forgiveness is the attribute of the strong.
context: Linking forgiveness to moral strength rather than weakness.

text: You must be the change you wish to see in the world.
context: The famous call to personal responsibility and example.

text: First they ignore you, then they laugh at you, then they fight you, then you win.
context: Describing the typical arc of nonviolent resistance.

text: Strength does not come from physical capacity. It comes from an indomitable will.
context: The source of power in nonviolent struggle.

text: Live as if you were to die tomorrow. Learn as if you were to live forever.
source: Various (often quoted in his educational writings)
context: Urgency of action combined with lifelong learning.

text: Happiness is when what you think, what you say, and what you do are in harmony.
context: The unity of thought, word, and deed as the foundation of a good life.

text: The future depends on what you do today.
context: Personal responsibility for the shape of tomorrow.

text: There is more to life than increasing its speed.
context: Critique of speed and materialism in Hind Swaraj and later talks.

text: I object to violence because when it appears to do good, the good is only temporary; the evil it does is permanent.
context: Moral case against violence even when it seems expedient.

text: Freedom is not worth having if it does not connote freedom to err.
context: True freedom includes the right to make mistakes.

text: The difference between what we do and what we are capable of doing would suffice to solve most of the world's problems.
context: The gap between current effort and true capacity.

text: Non-violence is the greatest force at the disposal of mankind. It is mightier than the mightiest weapon of destruction devised by the ingenuity of man.
context: Claiming the superior power of nonviolent resistance.

text: To give pleasure to a single heart by a single act is better than a thousand heads bowing in prayer.
context: Prioritizing concrete acts of kindness.

text: A 'No' uttered from the deepest conviction is better than a 'Yes' merely uttered to please, or worse, to avoid trouble.
context: The moral courage required to speak uncomfortable truth.

text: The greatness of a nation and its moral progress can be judged by the way its animals are treated.
context: Extending moral concern beyond humans.

text: Earth provides enough to satisfy every man's needs, but not every man's greed.
source: Various (widely attributed in his writings on trusteeship and simplicity)
context: Distinguishing need from greed; foundational to his economic thought.

text: In the midst of darkness, light persists.
speech: On hope during the independence struggle
context: Refusing to surrender to despair even in the darkest times of the movement.
